channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: US Strikes Iranian Military Sites, Tehran Retaliates Against American Base
item.description: The US military struck Iranian radar and drone control sites after Tehran shot down an American MQ-1 Predator drone. Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they targeted a US base in response, marking the latest escalation in the three-month-old conflict.

item.title: Israel Captures Strategic Beaufort Castle in Deeper Lebanon Incursion
item.description: Israeli troops captured the historic Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon as Netanyahu ordered a broader offensive against Hezbollah. The fighting represents the largest spillover from the Iran-US conflict.

item.title: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Oil Pipeline, Refinery and Fuel Depot
item.description: Ukrainian drones targeted energy infrastructure across multiple Russian regions overnight, hitting an oil pipeline pumping station, a refinery and a fuel depot in an escalating campaign against Russian energy assets.

item.title: Zelenskiy Seeks Peace Progress Before Winter as Ukraine Gains Strategic Ground
item.description: Ukraine's president said he wants to advance peace talks with Russia before winter, citing Kyiv's improved strategic position. The interview comes as Ukrainian forces continue drone strikes deep inside Russian territory.

item.title: Kuwait Intercepts Drone and Missile Attacks as Iran Conflict Spreads
item.description: Kuwait's military intercepted missiles and drones over the country as regional spillover from the Iran-US conflict intensifies. The Gulf state reported air defense activations amid broader escalation across the Middle East.

item.title: India Faces Critical RBI Policy Decision Amid Iran War Pressure
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India meets this week as the Iran conflict drives up energy prices and complicates growth and inflation forecasts for Asia's third-largest economy.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Takes Center Stage at Computex Taipei
item.description: Jensen Huang opens the Computex trade show in Taiwan with a major speech on AI products and the island's central role in the semiconductor industry.

item.title: Gold Slips as Markets Await Trump Decision on Iran Ceasefire
item.description: Gold prices fell as a stronger dollar and rising oil prices pressured the metal, with investors waiting for Trump's decision on extending the Iran ceasefire.

item.title: China Crude Oil Imports Slump to Near 10-Year Low
item.description: China's seaborne crude imports fell to the lowest in almost a decade as the Iran war reshuffles operations for the world's biggest oil importer.

item.title: US Castigates Europe Over Defense Spending as NATO Reassures Asia
item.description: The United States fired a fresh broadside at NATO allies in Singapore over defense spending, while Western European officials insisted the alliance remains resilient amid questions about American commitment.

item.title: Ethiopian Election Expected to Hand Abiy's Party Landslide Win
item.description: Ethiopia holds parliamentary and regional elections that analysts expect Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party to win in a landslide, despite significant unrest in much of the country.

item.title: US Moves to Halt Nvidia AI Chip Shipments to Chinese Firms Outside China
item.description: The US issued new guidance to block exports of advanced AI chips to subsidiaries of Chinese technology firms operating outside China, closing a potential loophole in export controls.

item.title: Explosion Kills at Least 55 in Myanmar, BBC Reports
item.description: A devastating explosion in Myanmar killed at least 55 people according to BBC reports, with details of the cause and location still emerging.

item.title: Czech Republic Likely to Miss NATO Defense Spending Target
item.description: Prime Minister Andrej Babis said the Czech Republic will probably miss NATO's target for military spending to reach 2% of GDP this year, amid broader questions about European defense commitments.

item.title: Syria's Sharaa Holds Phone Call With Trump, Presidency Says
item.description: Syria's presidency announced that leader Ahmed al-Sharaa held a phone call with President Trump, discussing potential sanctions relief and reconstruction efforts.

item.title: China Patrols Scarborough Shoal After Philippines Warns of Threat
item.description: China's military and coast guard carried out patrols near disputed waters in the South China Sea, a day after the Philippines said it remained under threat despite recent US-China tensions easing.

item.title: Japan Pushes Back Against Accusations of New Militarism
item.description: Japanese officials rejected criticism that the country is pursuing a new militarism, defending defense buildup plans amid regional tensions with China and North Korea.

item.title: South Korea and Japan Discuss Military Logistics Support Deal
item.description: South Korea and Japan are in discussions about a military logistics support agreement, Seoul announced, signaling deeper security cooperation amid regional challenges.

item.title: Patriarch of One of Moldova's Two Competing Churches Steps Down
item.description: The patriarch of one of Moldova's two competing Orthodox churches resigned, a split that reflects broader divisions in the country's international outlook between East and West.

item.title: Child Killed in Drone Attack on Russian-Held Kherson Region
item.description: A Russian-appointed official said a Ukrainian drone struck an apartment building in a Russian-held part of southern Kherson region, killing a child and injuring 11 people.

item.title: UK Businesses Stay Downbeat About Economy, Surveys Show
item.description: British businesses remained pessimistic about economic prospects according to new surveys, reflecting ongoing uncertainty about post-Brexit trade and global headwinds.

item.title: Trump's Iran Excursion Compared to Vietnam War Era
item.description: Analysis draws parallels between Trump's handling of the Iran conflict and historical patterns from the Vietnam War era, examining presidential decision-making in times of crisis.

item.title: Saudi Shares Gain, Qatar Slips on Iran-US Deal Uncertainty
item.description: Saudi Arabia's stock market closed higher while Qatar's slipped as investors reacted to ongoing uncertainty about whether the US and Iran will extend their ceasefire.

item.title: Oil Rises More Than 2% as Israel Moves Further Into Lebanon
item.description: Crude oil futures rose more than 2% as the Israel-Hezbollah conflict expands and Middle East supply concerns grow, with US crude reaching $89.53 per barrel.

item.title: Dollar Steadies as Markets Await Signals on Iran War
item.description: The US dollar held steady after weekly losses as markets watched for developments in Middle East peace talks and central bank policy signals.

item.title: Iran Nuclear Negotiations Back in Focus Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Iran is making changes to the latest draft of a memorandum of understanding with the US after Trump toughened the American stance, with nuclear program provisions at the center of negotiations.
